sources.title: Psychiatry: Mind, Brain, Person, Society
sources.locator: Course metadata and promise; sections 1–3, 5–6, 10–11, and 16; especially the integrated field statement and supervised-practice boundary

uses.rightsBasisEvidence: The lesson retains and adapts the syllabus's named mind, brain, person, relationship, culture, and system framing plus its explicit academic-versus-clinical boundary. ATTRIBUTION.md identifies the exact commit, CC BY 4.0 licence, retained material, and modifications. The lesson adds original cases, explanation, scope test, practice, transfer, and assessment.

agentAccess.notes: Read from the authorized local repository checkout. The exact commit's content licence permits adaptation with attribution and change notice; the public site robots file allows access, and no source-specific restriction on this agent use was found.

sources.title: High Quality Post-Graduate Training in Psychiatry
sources.version: WPA Position Statement GA 23.10.3; approved 30 September 2023; header states last reviewed October 2017
sources.locator: Pages 1–4: cultural context and lived experience; recommendations 8–19 on local systems, supervised development, public mental health, research, service-user involvement, and training variation
sources.license: No open licence displayed in the checked PDF; citation-only factual reference

uses.rightsBasisEvidence: Only bounded facts about the stated breadth, supervision, and international variation of postgraduate psychiatry training were used. No wording, table, recommendation text, case, graphic, or document structure was copied or adapted.

agentAccess.notes: The public robots file allowed general access subject to a ten-second crawl delay. A check of public site routes and the source page found no term prohibiting this single, non-bulk agent access; no login, paywall, rate limit, or technical control was bypassed. This is a dated observation, not a warranty.

sources.title: Psychiatry: Overview, principles, and initial certification requirements
creators: American Board of Psychiatry and Neurology
sources.publisher: American Board of Psychiatry and Neurology
sources.version: Current public page for the September 2026 initial-certification cycle
sources.locator: Psychiatry: Overview and Statement of Principles; Initial Certification sections A–B and active-license requirement
sources.license: Copyright American Board of Psychiatry and Neurology; all rights reserved; citation-only factual reference

uses.rightsBasisEvidence: Only facts establishing psychiatry's medical-specialty framing and one jurisdiction's separate physician-training, licensure, and certification route were used. No source wording, examination content, tables, media, or page structure was copied or adapted.

agentAccess.notes: The public robots file contained no disallow rule. The checked public terms page contained no automated-agent or AI-access prohibition found for this use. Copyright restrictions were honored by facts-only research and original expression; no login or technical control was bypassed.
